content.text:
Based on the chart, the key insight is that a "timeless" name — one consistently given over a long period — would have the **widest IQR** (interquartile range), since people with that name would span many age groups from young to old.

<think>
Looking at the scatter plot:
- X-axis = Median Age (0–80)
- Y-axis = Interquartile Range (0–70+)

A name given consistently across many decades would show up with a large IQR, because both young and old people share the name — making the 25th to 75th percentile spread very wide.

Lucille sits at the very top of the chart, with the highest IQR (~70 years), clearly above all other names including Alice, Josephine, Eleanor, and Hazel. This means people named Lucille span an extremely wide age range (from relatively young to very old), indicating the name has been given consistently over generations.
</think>

<answer>
Lucille — it has the highest Interquartile Range (~70 years) on the chart, meaning people named Lucille span the widest age distribution among living bearers of that name. A large IQR indicates the name has been consistently given across many generations, making it the most timeless.
</answer>
